message:
feat(extensions): parallel WASM dispatch, tiered timeouts, lockfile reconciliation

Addresses the three follow-ups split out from the #46 investigation.

#49: PackageManager::reconcile_all_lockfiles() prunes lockfile
  entries whose identity no longer appears in settings.json, called
  implicitly from ensure_packages_installed(). Reconciled removals
  are recorded in package-trust-audit.jsonl with
  reason_codes=["reconciled"] so the audit trail is preserved. Prune
  failures log and continue — stale entries are inert, so the
  fallback never blocks startup.

#50: add EXTENSION_INFO_EVENT_TIMEOUT_MS (500ms, strictly shorter
  than the 5s EXTENSION_EVENT_TIMEOUT_MS) plus
  ExtensionEventName::is_informational() / default_timeout_ms(),
  which classify lifecycle/telemetry events (turn_start, agent_end,
  message_*, tool_execution_*, model_select, user_bash, …) as
  fire-and-forget. dispatch_event() now uses the per-event default
  so a deadlocked info-only extension stalls for ~500ms instead of
  the full general budget. Actionable events (ToolCall,
  SessionBefore*, BeforeAgentStart, Context, Input,
  ResourcesDiscover) keep the full 5s budget. Fail-open semantics
  unchanged.

#51: dispatch_wasm_event_value() fans out across subscribed
  extensions concurrently via futures::future::join_all instead of
  awaiting sequentially. Each WasmExtensionHandle owns its own
  Arc<AsyncMutex<Instance>> so the join doesn't serialize on a
  shared lock. Total dispatch latency with N extensions and one
  timing out is now ~= per-extension timeout, not N × timeout. Error
  attribution is preserved — results carry the registration.name so
  failure logs still name the responsible extension.

Regression coverage:
- extensions::tests::informational_events_use_short_timeout /
  actionable_events_use_full_timeout / info_timeout_is_strictly_shorter_than_general_timeout
- package_manager::tests::reconcile_lockfile_prunes_entries_missing_from_settings /
  reconcile_lockfile_noop_when_lockfile_absent

Closes #49, #50, #51.
